codeReview.summary: The fix removes skipped VAL-EXEC-001 tests and keeps explicit VAL-EXEC-003 tagged provenance assertions present in the feature branch, but it still does not prove the required failed/cancelled terminal phase→packet-status mapping behavior. The new failed/cancelled tests assert mission result status only, leaving packet-level mapping and terminal completedAt semantics unverified.

issues.description: VAL-EXEC-001 remains incomplete for failed/cancelled terminal mappings: both new tests explicitly switch scope to RESULT status (comments at lines 2128-2132) and only assert `results.first?.status`/`runID` (lines 2196-2200 and 2269-2274), with no assertion that packet status transitions to `.failed`/`.cancelled` and no packet `completedAt` assertion. This does not satisfy the required failed/cancelled terminal phase→packet-status evidence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-orchestration worker skill remains misaligned with canonical validator commands, which can encourage inconsistent evidence collection.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/runtime-orchestration-worker/SKILL.md` still prescribes `OpenBurnBar*` filter commands, while `.factory/services.yaml` defines canonical `BurnBar*` commands. This rerun handoff also reports `swift test --package-path OpenBurnBarDaemon` full-suite execution instead of the feature's explicit focused verification matrix.

summary: Review failed. This rerun removes skip markers and preserves explicit VAL-EXEC-003 provenance coverage, but failed/cancelled VAL-EXEC-001 evidence is still scoped to result status instead of packet-level terminal mapping, so the core blocker is not fully resolved.
